jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening couch gag sequence with Homer likely crashing or being injured
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase paired with physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated into his character but predictable

jokes.text: You're watching PBS? - Hey, I'm as surprised as you...
jokes.setup: Homer watching PBS is unexpected given his character
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware admission of surprise
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor that plays on expectations about Homer's viewing habits

jokes.text: It's about a hard-drinking yet loving family of soccer hooligans. If they're not having a go with a bird... they're having a row with a wanker. Cheeky.
jokes.punchline: Excessive use of British slang culminating in 'Cheeky'
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of British TV and American stereotypes of British culture

jokes.text: Classic. Not hard to see why it's England's longest-running series.
jokes.punchline: Homer's deadpan assessment of its quality and longevity
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic commentary on lowbrow entertainment being popular

jokes.text: you're a thief- a common thief!
jokes.punchline: Betty White's aggressive accusation
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Betty White's wholesome image and aggressive tone

jokes.text: Sorry, but these thieves make me so damn mad. You know who you are. Thieves!
jokes.punchline: Betty White doubling down with profanity and direct address
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Betty White character bit with unexpected aggression

jokes.text: Or this umbrella, featuring a picture of our classic tote bag.
jokes.punchline: Umbrella with picture of tote bag is absurdly meta
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd PBS premium gift that satirizes their desperate fundraising tactics

jokes.text: Why are you torturing me? I'm just a man!
jokes.setup: Extended PBS pledge drive interrupting his show
jokes.punchline: Homer's melodramatic plea
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic overdramatic response to minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Well, why didn't you say so? Yes, I'd like to pledge $10,000... to get them to shut up. From... anonymous. Done and done.
jokes.setup: PBS needs $10,000 to return to programming
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately pledges money he doesn't have just to stop the interruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer impulsivity - willing to pledge enormous sum for immediate gratification

jokes.text: Aw, how they gonna find me?
jokes.setup: Marge points out he doesn't have $10,000
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive confidence in anonymity
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer short-sighted thinking and overconfidence

jokes.text: But thanks to Insta-Trace... we've learned it's Homer Simpson of 742 Evergreen Terrace.
jokes.punchline: Technology immediately reveals Homer's identity and address
jokes.explanation: Perfectly undermines Homer's confidence with modern technology plot device

jokes.text: Why did I register with Insta-Trace?
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-incriminating admission
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic self-sabotage through his own past decisions

jokes.text: That's it, boy! Go get help. [Bart swings away]
jokes.setup: Homer tells Bart to get help
jokes.punchline: Bart literally swings away on playground equipment instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Great visual subversion of expectation with Bart taking instruction literally but unhelpfully

jokes.text: They don't show police chases, do they?
jokes.punchline: Marge's assumption it must be law enforcement related
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - practical and long-suffering

jokes.text: especially now, when the rich mosaic of cable programming... has made public television so very, very unnecessary.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming it's an honor to donate to PBS
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining PBS by calling it unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire delivered with Homer's characteristic foot-in-mouth syndrome

jokes.text: Maybe your movie star banks are open crazy hours... but we in Springfield are simple folk. We like our cars fast and our banks closed.
jokes.setup: TV crew expects banks to be open at 12:15
jokes.punchline: Homer's folksy explanation with absurd priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that satirizes small-town attitudes while being genuinely quotable

jokes.text: Yeah. It's a real ghost town in there. Get in there.
jokes.punchline: Bank is clearly busy, Homer's obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Simple visual contradiction of Homer's previous claim

jokes.text: 'You are on television. Please play along.' Are you robbing me?
jokes.setup: Note asking teller to play along
jokes.punchline: Teller's confusion about whether it's a robbery
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with realistic confusion from civilian character

jokes.text: I can't do it. I can't kill a man.
jokes.punchline: Homer's melodramatic confession about not being able to kill
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic overdramatization of a simple financial transaction

jokes.text: And you thought you could stab your problems away? Silly goose.
jokes.punchline: Condescending 'silly goose' after mentioning stabbing
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect tonal whiplash from violence to condescension

jokes.text: The hooligans! Bash his eyes out!
jokes.punchline: They're the violent British hooligans from the TV show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that brings the fictional TV show into reality - excellent structure and payoff

jokes.text: It's a beautiful day to kick your ass!
jokes.punchline: Cheerful delivery of violent threat
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of British hooligan character with juxtaposition of pleasant tone and violent content

jokes.text: Sanctuary! Sanctuary!
jokes.punchline: Quasimodo reference from Hunchback of Notre Dame
jokes.explanation: Classic cultural reference that fits Homer's tendency to quote movies/literature inappropriately

jokes.text: Oh, why did I teach him that word?
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's regret at educating Homer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reaction that explains Homer's knowledge while showing Lovejoy's regret

jokes.text: Their bloodthirsty pursuit is made possible by a grant from the Chubb Group.
jokes.punchline: PBS sponsorship acknowledgment for their own violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that satirizes PBS sponsorship announcements while being perfectly timed and original

jokes.text: Give us the money! Elmo knows where you live.
jokes.punchline: Innocent children's character used as threat
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that subverts beloved children's character into something menacing with perfect economy

jokes.text: Give us the money! - Elmo knows where you live.
jokes.punchline: Elmo as menacing debt collector
jokes.explanation: Dark subversion of beloved children's character - economical and memorable

jokes.text: The word is 'drive.' Sure, sure. Bob's your uncle.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual agreement with British expression
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to British expressions from earlier, shows Homer picking up phrases

jokes.text: Nothing to see here, people. Just headed down to the dump with these children's letters to God.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy trying to hide Homer's escape
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions disposing of children's prayers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that reveals religious hypocrisy while perfectly fitting Lovejoy's character

jokes.text: I'm no missionary. I don't even believe in 'Jebus.'
jokes.setup: Homer protesting being sent as missionary
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Jesus as 'Jebus'
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer mispronunciation that became a cultural touchstone - perfect character integration

jokes.text: Oh, save me, Jebus!
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling on the 'Jebus' he just denied believing in
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback showing Homer's hypocrisy and desperation with great timing

jokes.text: Micro-Asia? That's 12,000 miles away.
jokes.punchline: Fictional location with absurd distance
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist geography joke but not particularly memorable or well-crafted

jokes.text: He's dead, isn't he?
jokes.setup: Learning Homer went on missionary work
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate assumption of death
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Marge's realistic assessment of Homer's survival skills

jokes.text: Homer to Jebus. [Moans]
jokes.punchline: Still calling out to 'Jebus' on radio
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Jebus joke, maintains consistency but not particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: First of all, forget everything you learned in missionary school. Done.
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately confirms he's forgotten everything
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's eagerness to avoid responsibility and education

jokes.text: Me Homer. Me hiding from PBS.
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission of his real reason for being there
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic honesty and inability to maintain a cover story

jokes.text: We cannot read.
jokes.setup: Homer hands them a Bible to learn from
jokes.punchline: Simple statement revealing the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reveal of Homer's thoughtless approach to missionary work

jokes.text: Does the word 'jet lag' mean anything to you people?
jokes.setup: Natives asking Homer to teach them
jokes.punchline: Homer expecting primitive people to understand modern travel concepts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's cultural insensitivity and self-centeredness

jokes.text: Hey, what happened to all the shirtless girls... you see in all the geographical magazines?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate expectations from National Geographic stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriate comment that reveals his shallow motivations and cultural ignorance

jokes.text: Craig and Amy gave us the gift of shame.
jokes.punchline: Describing Western modesty as 'the gift of shame'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical commentary on Western missionary work and cultural imperialism with perfect economy

jokes.text: All the naked women are on that island. Yeah, anything goes over there. Bouncy, bouncy.
jokes.setup: Native explaining about the other island
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate perverted interest with childish expression
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer perversion with juvenile delivery, fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Great. Now my testicle's got ants on it.
jokes.setup: Earthquake during Homer eating ox testicle
jokes.punchline: Homer's disgusted complaint about ants on his food
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's mildly effective but relies on cheap shock value

jokes.text: I'm gonna call you Lisa Jr.
jokes.setup: Native girl gives very long, complex name
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately simplifies to familiar reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's cultural insensitivity and tendency to simplify everything to his own experience

jokes.text: Craig and Amy, Craig and Amy. Why don't you just marry Craig and Amy?
jokes.setup: Natives keep mentioning Craig and Amy's good works
jokes.punchline: Homer's jealous, childish response
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer jealousy and immaturity when compared unfavorably to others

jokes.text: Oh, man, I need a beer.
jokes.setup: Learning there's no TV or couches
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate need for alcohol to cope
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer response but perfectly fits his character's dependence on creature comforts

jokes.text: Oh, God, oh, God, oh, God, oh, God! [Together] Oh, God, oh, God, oh, God, oh, God!
jokes.punchline: Natives joining in, thinking it's a prayer or ritual
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing cultural misunderstanding and how Homer inadvertently influences others

jokes.text: You've only been gone two days. Really? Without TV it's hard to know... when one day begins and the other ends.
jokes.setup: Homer claims first month was rough
jokes.punchline: Homer's dependence on TV for basic time awareness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment and social commentary on modern media dependence

jokes.text: I just don't think it's funny.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining Marge won't let him read Hägar the Horrible
jokes.punchline: Marge's simple, honest assessment of the comic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's practical, no-nonsense approach to humor

jokes.text: Bart, you're the man of the house. Lisa, I'm promoting you to boy. Maggie's now the brainy girl. Toaster can fill in for Maggie. And, Marge, you're a consultant.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd role assignments including toaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity that captures Homer's logical gaps and family dynamics perfectly

jokes.text: I got some civilization to spread like butter... on the English muffin that is these people... with all their little nooks and crannies.
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor comparing people to English muffins
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor that's slightly overworked but captures Homer's peculiar way of expressing ideas

jokes.text: Dad, are you licking toads? I'm not not licking toads.
jokes.punchline: Homer's non-denial denial
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Homer's evasiveness and implies he's definitely licking hallucinogenic toads

jokes.text: Like this, from the book of... 'P-salms.'
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'Psalms' with hard P
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mispronunciation showing his unfamiliarity with religious texts

jokes.text: Well, not the Unitarians. If that's the one true faith, I'll eat my hat.
jokes.setup: Native asking which religion is right
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive attitude toward Unitarians
jokes.explanation: Good religious humor that pokes fun at perceived liberal Protestant denominations

jokes.text: Well, Ak, it's because God is powerful, but also insecure- like Barbra Streisand before James Brolin. Oh, he's been a rock.
jokes.setup: Ak asking why God cares about worship
jokes.punchline: Comparing God to insecure celebrity with oddly specific reference
jokes.explanation: Creative theological interpretation with unexpected celebrity comparison that somehow works

jokes.text: Now, if The Flintstones has taught us anything... it's that pelicans can be used to mix cement.
jokes.setup: Homer applying modern knowledge to primitive setting
jokes.punchline: Using cartoon logic as factual reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations - using cartoon as educational reference shows Homer's warped understanding of reality

jokes.text: Come on, little friend. Make a wisecrack. You know- 'It's a living.' That sort of thing.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to use pelican like in Flintstones
jokes.punchline: Expecting real bird to deliver cartoon catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of Flintstones logic with Homer expecting reality to match cartoon conventions

jokes.text: In 10 short years you've caused 17 meltdowns. One is too many.
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to absurd number of nuclear disasters
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about nuclear safety with perfect deadpan delivery and escalating absurdity

jokes.text: You sold weapons-grade plutonium to the Iraqis- with no markup.
jokes.punchline: Burns is upset about the lack of profit, not the treason
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Burns' twisted priorities - more concerned with profit margins than national security

jokes.text: And worst of all... you took the Hamburglar's birthday off last Monday and Wednesday.
jokes.punchline: Fictional character's birthday is somehow the worst offense, mentioned twice
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke where the absurd becomes more important than the serious, with great specificity

jokes.text: The magic medicine of... casino gambling!
jokes.setup: Homer promising modern-day miracle to revitalize communities
jokes.punchline: Gambling as the solution, presented as medicine
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of how gambling is promoted as economic development with medical metaphor

jokes.text: Now over here I've brewed up my favorite beverage. Beer. It's mostly Dristan and holy water, but it does the job.
jokes.punchline: Absurd ingredients of cold medicine and holy water
jokes.explanation: Good Homer ingenuity joke with absurd but somehow logical ingredients for his purposes

jokes.text: And who knows? Maybe you'll hit the jackpot, get off this island... and spend the rest of your days in a tropical paradise.
jokes.punchline: They're already in a tropical paradise but he's selling escape from it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony showing Homer's complete misunderstanding of their situation and Western consumer culture

jokes.text: How can ace be one and 11? Huh? What kind of god would allow that?
jokes.punchline: Blaming God for confusing card game rules
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of gambling addiction leading to existential crisis over arbitrary game rules

jokes.text: I gave you a glittering Vegas and you turned it into a scanty Atlantic City.
jokes.setup: Homer's casino venture has gone wrong
jokes.punchline: Comparing failed result to less glamorous gambling destination
jokes.explanation: Good geographic humor contrasting gambling destinations and Homer's disappointed expectations

jokes.text: I don't even like macaroni salad... but look at me! [Chomping]
jokes.setup: Native explaining how Homer's buffets corrupted them
jokes.punchline: Addictive behavior with food they don't even enjoy
jokes.explanation: Good representation of compulsive behavior and how Western vices corrupt innocence

jokes.text: Our island has not been this damaged since the A-bomb tests.
jokes.setup: Native describing damage from Homer's influence
jokes.punchline: Comparing to nuclear testing as reference point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that provides historical context while showing scale of Homer's destructive influence

jokes.text: That's it, Bart. You're taking this 'man of the house' thing too far. You're right. I'm sorry. Tell you what. Saturday night, we'll go out for steaks- just you and me.
jokes.setup: Bart acting like stereotypical dismissive husband
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverting to child needing mother's approval
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Bart's inability to maintain adult behavior when challenged

jokes.text: Since when? Since I got here. Now either grab a stone or go to hell.
jokes.setup: Native asking when they became terrible sinners
jokes.punchline: Homer's arbitrary timeline and ultimatum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - creating problems then offering harsh binary solutions with no middle ground

jokes.text: Well, I may not know much about God... but I have to say, we built a pretty nice cage for him.
jokes.punchline: Describing church as cage for God
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irreverent take on religious architecture that's both theologically provocative and character-perfect

jokes.text: And I'm in a gambling program. For real this time.
jokes.setup: Native expressing gratitude for having place to pray
jokes.punchline: Admission of repeated failed attempts at gambling recovery
jokes.explanation: Standard addiction recovery joke with implied history of failure, moderately effective

jokes.text: Every Sunday for the rest of our lives. [Laughs] No, really.
jokes.setup: Native asking how often they must attend church
jokes.punchline: Homer treating weekly church as joke, then confirming it's serious
jokes.explanation: Good delivery joke showing Homer's casual relationship with religious commitment

jokes.text: But God's palace is way up on the moon. So if you want him to hear us... we've gotta crank up the volume.
jokes.punchline: Absurd theology placing God on the moon requiring louder worship
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist theology that perfectly captures Homer's literal, mechanical understanding of spirituality

jokes.text: Homer's doin' your work! I'm the greatest missionary of all time!
jokes.punchline: Homer's massive ego about minimal religious work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer arrogance, claiming unearned religious authority

jokes.text: I'm the greatest missionary of all time!
jokes.setup: Homer's pride in his religious work
jokes.punchline: Self-aggrandizing declaration just before disaster strikes
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate comeuppance with Homer's typical overconfidence and poor timing

jokes.text: Oh, that Homer- always getting into trouble. And if you're one of the millions who enjoys his adventures- or should I say misadventures-
jokes.punchline: Self-aware correction to 'misadventures'
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor that sets up the closing pledge drive parody but isn't particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: Sure, Fox makes a fortune from advertising... but it's still not enough. Not nearly enough!
jokes.setup: Parodying PBS pledge drives for Fox
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging corporate greed with escalating emphasis
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on commercial television's relationship with money, bringing the PBS parody full circle

jokes.text: Hello! Murdoch here. $10,000! You've saved my network. Wouldn't be the first time.
jokes.punchline: Murdoch's casual reference to being saved financially before
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's original $10,000 pledge while satirizing media mogul financial troubles
